qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: App v1 detail endpoints allow low-privilege users to read and modify foreign intervention resources
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an app-v1 object-level authorization flaw in mobile API record handlers, not the advisory's P7M filename injection into `src/Util/XML.php::decodeP7M()`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Invoice import .p7m filename reaches shell exec and permits command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable import flow and the same unsanitized `src/Util/XML.php::decodeP7M()` `exec()` sink named by the advisory, with attacker-controlled `.p7m` filenames reaching shell evaluation through `FatturaElettronica`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Invoice import upload trusts multipart filename and permits arbitrary file write via path traversal
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding shares the import surface but its root cause is unchecked path concatenation into `file_put_contents()`, not the advisory's `.p7m` filename injection into `XML::decodeP7M()` and `exec()`.

selected_finding.title: Invoice import .p7m filename reaches shell exec and permits command execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's vulnerable code and root cause because attacker-controlled `.p7m` filenames from the import flow reach `Util\XML::decodeP7M()` and are interpolated into the same `exec()` command described in the advisory. The evidence is static and session-log based only, so the match is advisory-correct but unsupported by runnable PoC artifacts in this session.
evidence: The advisory identifies `src/Util/XML.php:100` `decodeP7M()` as the vulnerable `exec()` sink, and the selected finding cites the same file, function, and unsanitized `$file` interpolation.

Preserved source output in `logs/execution_history.jsonl` shows `plugins/importFE/actions.php` using the raw uploaded filename, `FatturaElettronica::__construct()` calling `XML::decodeP7M()` for `.p7m` names, and `src/Util/XML.php` executing `openssl smime -verify ... -in "$file" ...`.

The other qualifying findings describe different mechanisms: unchecked file-path write via `file_put_contents()` and app-v1 authorization bypasses, neither of which is the advisory's vulnerable code path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: The selected finding folder has no `poc_report.json`, no `reproduction/` directory, and no `reproduction/run.sh` to execute under `.judge/poc_runs/`.

Session artifacts state the dry-run workspace lacked `repo/vendor` dependencies and a configured application or database instance, so the advisory-specific import flow was not runnable for predicate-based runtime confirmation.
